titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Fishing Manuscripts Collection
titlestmt.author: by Beinecke Staff

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Saturday November 3, 2018 at 03:34

did.unittitle: Fishing manuscripts collection

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

did.abstract: Collection consists of manuscripts, typescripts, photographs, and other documents relating to fishing, flies for fishing, and fishing trips taken in England and North America. Most of the items were formerly in the collection of David Wagstaff.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Gift of Isabelle Tilford Wagstaff, 1951-1953. Purchased from Wormser on the David Wagstaff Memorial Fund, 1952. Gift of Vernon S. Hidy, 1968, 1975. Gift of Lindley Eberstadt, 1969. Purchased from Henry Bristow on the Jonathan O. Bulkley Fund, 1994. Purchased from Rodolphe Chamonal on the David Wagstaff Fund, 2003. For further information see Collections Contents list.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The materials are open for research.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The Fishing Manuscripts Collection is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Fishing Manuscripts Collection. General Collection, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Descriptions in the Collections Contents list were taken from existing catalogue cards.
Former call numbers: MS Vault Shelves Fishing, Uncat MS Vault 756, Uncas MSS 553

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The Fishing Manuscripts Collection consists of manuscripts, typescripts, photographs, and other documents relating to fishing, flies for fishing, and fishing trips taken. Most of the items were formerly in the collection of David Wagstaff (1882-1951).

bioghist.head: David Wagstaff (1882-1951)
bioghist.p: David Wagstaff, broker, sportsman and collector of sporting books, paintings and prints, served as the Curator of Sporting Books in the Yale University Library from 1943 to 1951.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged by previously assigned consecutive numbers.

did.unittitle: Fishing Manuscripts
1. Directions for making Artificial Flies, by Samuel Jemmat of Devonshire, Esq., given to me by his son

odd.head: General note
odd.p: Manuscript in unidentified hand, bound in three-quarters green leather with marbled boards; 8 leaves, 16.5 cm.
With bookplate of Dean Sage.

did.unittitle: 2. Flies for the Rivers Exe, Devon, & Barle, Somerset, by I. N. B.

odd.head: General note
odd.p: Manuscript in unidentified hand, bound in vellum envelope cover; 25 leaves, 15 cm..
With signature of H. M. Waugh, HMS Kent, and engraved book ticket of John Brown, stationer of London.

did.unittitle: 3. Of Artificial Flies and the Seasons proper for using them, by George Cole Bainbridge.

odd.head: General note
odd.p: Autograph manuscript; bound in red roan, with case; 124 pages, 18 cm.
At end are four leaves containing watercolor sketches of insects and a pencil sketch of a river scene with castle.
"The original manuscript material for The Fly-Fisher's Guide " (Dean Sage, Catalogue of Books on Angling ).
Laid in are several autograph memoranda and other memoranda (dating from 1778-1841) in several unidentified hands. Author's autograph inscription dated 1812 inside front cover.
With bookplate of Dean Sage. With a note in David Wagstaff's hand: "Tayleure sale, 1828, 281"; from the Grego collection

did.unittitle: 4. Angling commonplace books (3 volumes)

odd.head: General note
odd.p: Manuscript in an unidentified hand, with some clippings pasted in; 18-20.5 cm.

did.unittitle: 5. General Observations to be Understood by all such who desire to attain to the Compleat and perfect art of Angling

odd.head: General note
odd.p: Manuscript written in an unidentified hand, bound in black morocco with cover title: Old M.S.S. on angling; dated 1763 on flyleaf, in an unidentified hand; 48 pages, 24.5 cm.
With two bookplates of John Gerard Heckscher, and autograph presentation inscription from F. Wright to F. M. Walhan, dated June 4, 1892

did.unittitle: 6. Journal of Miss Peggy Harrison on the Ristigouche, June 1929

odd.head: General note
odd.p: Typescript, 1 of 2 copies, bound in tan buckram; 45 leaves; 28 cm.
Accompanied by an autograph letter signed from George Harrison (1872-1956) of St. Davids, Pennsylvania, to David Wagstaff, concerning the manuscript about his daughter's trip, dated July 9, 1930.

did.unittitle: 7. The Baby Goes A-Fishing, by Cyrus Townsend Brady

odd.head: General note
odd.p: Autograph manuscript, bound in three-quarter red morocco, with marbled boards; 10 pages, 26 cm.
With bookplate of David Wagstaff

did.unittitle: 8. The Nipigon River: account of a fishing trip in the summer of 1873

odd.head: General note
odd.p: Manuscript in unidentified hand, likely that of Philadelphia lawyer George Sharswood, with nine photographs bound in as plates; bound in three-quarters green morocco; 64 pages, 24 cm.
With bookplate of George Sharswood

did.unittitle: 9. The Angler: bibliographical notes, mainly concerning works on angling and archery, written in an unidentified hand.

odd.head: General note
odd.p: Manuscript, bound in three-quarters green calf, with marbled boards and spine title: The Angler. M.S.S. Walton and Cotton, Etc.; 50 leaves, 23.5 cm
With bookplate of Dean Sage

did.unittitle: 10. Of Fishing

odd.head: General note
odd.p: Manuscript in an eighteenth century hand, prefaced by a prayer in another hand; bound by Rivière; 180 pages, 19 cm.
With bookplate of David Wagstaff

did.unittitle: 11. The Angler: a poem in ten cantos, by Thomas Pike Lathy

odd.head: General note
odd.p: Autograph manuscript of the volume published by W. Wright in 1819; 115 leaves, 24 cm.
With bookplates of Alfred Denison, Thomas Gosden, and David Wagstaff

did.unittitle: 12. Jottings Down, by one of our trouting party

odd.head: General note
odd.p: Manuscript by an unidentified author from New York, about his fishing trip to the Adirondacks with three friends from Boston, dated June 1858; 18 pages, 32.5 cm.

did.unittitle: 13. Papers relating to David Wagstaff's membership in the Ristigouche Salmon Club

separatedmaterial.head: Separated Materials
separatedmaterial.p: Documents were removed from this collection and added to David Wagstaff Papers (GEN MSS 415).

did.unittitle: 14. Noted Angling Resorts, by Wakeman Holberton

odd.head: General note
odd.p: Pen and ink drawings (31) with title page, index, and a watercolor frontispiece; contains images of noted fishing spots in New York, Pennsylvania, Maine, and Quebec; bound in citron morocco with gilt borders and dentelles; 31 pages, 28 cm.

did.unittitle: 15. The Art of angling

odd.head: General note
odd.p: Manuscript in an unidentified hand, bound in light brown calf; 76 pages, 16.5 cm.
Bound in at front is a half leaf with the following headline: "A Craft to take pykes &c: [From an old ms. on vellum, written probably about 1550, in my [Thomas Gosden?] own possession]."
On leaf preceding first page of manuscript, in another seventeenth century hand, "Natales muorum": 15 lines of genealogical notes.
With bookplates of William V. Guise and David Wagstaff

did.unittitle: 16. A Poetical Epistle from M.A.S. to Andrew Corbet of Acton Reyland Esq. on Fishing and Angling, by Moreton Aglionby Slaney

odd.head: General note
odd.p: Manuscript with one pen and pencil sketch, bound in three-quarters green calf, with marbled boards; 49 pages, 24 cm.
Slaney (1765-1817) was the brother-in-law of Sir Andrew Corbet.
With bookplate of David Wagstaff.

did.unittitle: 17. A Treatise of the Art of Taking Pike, Pearch, Chub &c, with the Trimmer to which is added a description of the Necessary Tackle together with directions for its use &c &c., by Thomas Gosden [?]

odd.head: General note
odd.p: Manuscript with a few pencil sketches, bound in three-quarters green morocco, with marbled boards; 27 leaves, 26 cm.
With bookplates of Alfred Denison and David Wagstaff.

did.unittitle: 18. A Chronological List of English Books on Angling, 1496-1917

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Wormser on the David Wagstaff Memorial Fund, 1952

odd.head: General note
odd.p: Manuscript in an unidentified hand, bound in brown half morocco; 331 pages, 21 cm.
Autograph of C. O. P. Gibson, Bywell Castle, Stocksfield, Northumberland (October 1923) and bookplate of John Wayland Leslie, Combe Court

did.unittitle: 19. Salmon Problems, by John William Willis Bund

odd.head: General note
odd.p: Typescript, bound in maroon morocco; 325 pages, 24 cm.
Willis Bund (1843-1928) was the chairman of the Severn Fishing Board.

did.unittitle: 20. Miscellaneous notes relating to Thomas Barker and his Art of Angling , by James Williamson.

odd.head: General note
odd.p: Manuscripts tipped into notebook bound in three-quarter green morocco with marbled boards, possibly by Thomas Gosden; 59 pages, 26 cm.

Includes Williamson's autograph manuscript copies of his letter to Samuel Tudor, May 16, 1816; two letters to Thomas Loxdale, August 9, 1814, and October 28, 1817; and letter to Samuel Bagster, May 28, 1809.
Laid in: Index to "Barker's Delight"; manuscript, undated
With bookplates of Alfred Denison and David Wagstaff.

did.unittitle: 21. The Leisenring Color & Materials Book for Fly Tying, by James E. Leisenring

odd.head: General note
odd.p: A pocket reference for fly fishermen designed by James E. Leisenring (1878-1951). Feathers, furs, herls & quills from the Leisenring Collection, assembled under the direction of V. S. Hidy.
One of eight sets of colors and materials, with manuscript title page; 16 pages, 18 cm.

did.unittitle: 22. Hunting and Fishing in Northern New York, by Frank French

odd.head: General note
odd.p: Twenty-four photographs bound in brown leather, with a pencil sketch of Frederick H. Lum by Dr. French as a frontispiece; 21 cm.
"No. 3 of 6 sets. F. H. Lum"

did.unittitle: 23. Addenda, by John Hayford Thorold

odd.head: General note
odd.p: Autograph manuscript; 188 pages, 22 cm.
Pages 138-152: "Fish taken at Syston, 1806-1827"
Pages 53-137 and 154-188 are blank

did.unittitle: 24. Angling journals

odd.head: General note
odd.p: Two manuscript journals recording kinds and quantities of catch, and companions present.

did.unittitle: 25. Fishing

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Henry Bristow on the Jonathan O. Bulkley Fund, 1994.

odd.head: General note
odd.p: Manuscript in an unidentified hand, with sketches of flies, ties, tackles, and fish, and a list of books related to fishing; 176 pages, 15.5 cm.
With notes and sketches at the end, in an unidentified hand.
Contains stamps of Low Moss Farm, Broughton-in-Furness; R. Tyson Johnstone; autograph of Jno. Dodson Skeleton, London, 1850; binder's ticket of J Sloper, stationer and bookbinder, London.

did.unittitle: Manuel de la Pèche à la ligne

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Rodolphe Chamonal on the David Wagstaff Fund, 2003.

odd.head: General note
odd.p: French translation of Edward Fitzgibbon's volume A Handbook of Angling: teaching fly-fishing, trolling, bottom-fishing, and salmon-fishing; with the natural history of river fish, and the best modes of catching them. By Ephemera , third edition, with sketches.
Bound manuscript signed B. Sautereau; 281 pages; 22 cm.
With bookplate of David Wagstaff.

did.unittitle: On fishing flies, by John Jackson of Tanfield Mill, May 1853, copied by James E. Leisenring

odd.head: General note
odd.p: Bound autograph manuscript; 30 pages; 14 cm.
From the James E. Leisenring Papers.

did.unittitle: The Art of Tying the Wet Fly, by James E. Leisenring

odd.head: General note
odd.p: Autograph manuscript bound into two sections: "Tying Fishing Flies" (91 pages) and "The Mechanical End of Tying the Artificial Trout Fishing Fly" (76 pages); 21.5 cm.
Published as The Art of Tying the Wet Fly , by James E. Leisenring, as told to V. S. Hidy (1941).

did.unittitle: Oversize
8. The Nipigon River: account of a fishing trip in the summer of 1873
14. Noted Angling Resorts, by Wakeman Holberton
